title: Pentas xanthorrhoea
taxonomy: Pentas xanthorrhoea is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, and it is commonly known as the "Egyptian star cluster" or "Egyptian pentas". The classification of Pentas xanthorrhoea is still being researched, but it is generally considered to be a member of the Apocynaceae family within the order Gentianales. More specifically, some sources place Pentas xanthorrhoea within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. However, further research may be needed to confirm its exact classification within the Apocynaceae family.
